mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive month-over-month RSAFS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 6.0% signals a tight labor market that boosts consumer confidence and wage-driven spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 6.0%, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate indicate a robust labor market expansion that is stronger than either signal alone, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive PAYEMS and negative UNRATE changes do not produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate raise short-term discount rates and reduce the present value of future corporate cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During identified tightening cycles, a 3-month rolling window shows positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns following month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,000 thousand units signals strong residential investment and construction sector momentum, which spills over into broader economic demand and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1,000, subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 45% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates negative supply shocks and contracting capacity utilization, which compresses corporate margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Following month-over-month INDPRO declines, positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer prices and a stable or declining federal funds rate compresses real interest rates, stimulating equity risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in predicting 60-day return direction over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals broad-based aggregate demand expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth coincides with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.
